title: Pre-Qualification Requirements. Applicants for issuance of a license shall
content: 59 submit the following documents/requirements for pre-qualification: 60 61 10 A. Business Name Certificate issued by the Department of Trade and Industry (DTI), in 1 the case of a single proprietorship; or a certified copy of Securities and Exchange 2 Commission (SEC) Registration, together with a certified copy of the Articles of 3 Partnership or Articles of Incorporation duly registered with the SEC in the case of a 4 partnership or corporation, respectively; 5 6 B. Proof of financial capacity: 7 8 i. Bank certificate showing a deposit of at least Five Million Pesos (Php 9 5,000,000.00), with authority to examine the bank account; 10 11 ii. Duly filed Income Tax Returns (ITR) for the last two (2) years of the proprietor, 12 partners, members of the Board of Directors and major stockholders of a 13 corporation and official receipts showing payment of income tax; and 14 15 iii. Proof of sources of investment. 16 17 C. Proof of existence of new principal: 18 19 i. MWO verified and/or consulate authenticated MA concluded by the manning 20 agency and the new principal. 21 22 ii. Principal’s profile to include: 23 24 a. Valid business license or commercial registration of the principal with 25 English translation; 26 b. Information on business activities; 27 c. Number of years in operation; and 28 d. Number of seafarers; 29 30 D. Proof of possession by the sole proprietor, managing partner, president or chief 31 executive officer, as the case may be, of a bachelor's degree, or at least four (4) years 32 experience in shipping-related or human resource management or experience in 33 heading or managing a shipping or manpower business. 34 35 E. Valid clearances from the NBI and the Department for the proprietor, partners, and all 36 members of the board of directors of the applicant agency. 37 38 If a member of the board is a foreign national, the following shall be required: a duly 39 authenticated police clearance from his/her country of origin, and an affidavit declaring 40 no criminal conviction or no pending criminal case for illegal recruitment or a crime 41 involving moral turpitude. 42 43 F. In case of a corporation or partnership, a duly notarized undertaking by the corporate 44 officers and directors, or partners, that they shall be jointly and severally liable with 45 the corporation or partnership for claims and/or damages that may be awarded to the 46 seafarers; 47 48 G. List of all officials and personnel involved in recruitment and placement, together with 49 their appointment, signed bio-data and two (2) copies of their passport-size pictures, 50 as well as their clearances from the NBI and their individual affidavits declaring that 51 they have no conviction or pending criminal case for illegal recruitment or case 52 involving moral turpitude; 53 54 H. Certificate of attendance of the sole proprietor, managing partner, president, chief 55 executive officer and/or operations manager in the Pre-Licensing Orientation Seminar 56 (PLOS); 57 58 I. Flowchart detailing the step-by-step recruitment procedures, documentary 59 requirements, briefings and orientations required, authorized fees and costs, 60 deployment timeframes, and the responsible officer/s and process cycle time for every 61 phase of the process; and 62 11 J. Four-year business plan detailing financial, market and operational viability, including 1 projected income and a risk management plan. 2 3 The Department, in accordance with law and after consultation with the stakeholders, 4 may modify the list or prescribe additional pre-qualification requirements. 5 6
